description: The operation completed successfully.
fix: No action is required as the process completed with status code 0.

description: Incorrect function. The requested system call, API, or driver function is invalid, unconfigured, or not implemented in the current execution context.
fix: Verify that the calling function is supported by the target operating system version.
Check driver configuration and ensure system updates are fully applied.
Ensure API parameters conform to standard calling conventions.

description: The system cannot find the file specified.
fix: Verify the accuracy of the target file path, including the directory path and file extension.
Check if the file has been deleted, moved, or restricted by active antivirus scanners.
Validate user access permissions on the parent directory containing the file.

description: The system cannot find the path specified.
fix: Confirm the logical or physical directory structure exists in the storage path.
Correct any spelling errors or incorrect directory separators in the file path.
Ensure that network-mounted paths or external drives are properly attached and mapped.

description: The system cannot open the file due to limits on open file descriptors or active file handles.
fix: Close inactive applications and terminate unnecessary background operations.
Refactor application code to ensure file handles are explicitly closed in finally blocks.
Increase maximum handle or file descriptor limits in the registry or application configurations.

description: Access is denied due to insufficient user security privileges or resource lock conditions.
fix: Execute the application or process using elevated administrative privileges (Run as Administrator).
Verify and modify security permissions on the target folder or file using NTFS properties.
Ensure the resource is not locked by another active process or system security policy.

description: The handle provided is invalid or has already been closed by another system thread.
fix: Debug application code to ensure the file or process handle is initialized before use.
Prevent double-closing or premature release of standard input/output handles.
Verify synchronization between parallel processing threads that share handle variables.

description: Not enough memory resources are available to process this command. The heap allocations cannot locate available memory block headers.
fix: Close memory-intensive system processes to free up system RAM.
Increase the system virtual memory pagefile allocation bounds in System Properties.
Optimize application code structures to reduce peak heap requirements and resolve memory leaks.

description: An attempt was made to load a program with an incorrect format.
fix: Verify that the application binary matches the operating system architecture (32-bit vs. 64-bit).
Reinstall the corrupted application to recover missing file blocks.
Confirm that dependent DLL packages are correctly registered and compatible.

description: Not enough storage is available to complete this operation. The virtual address space is exhausted.
fix: Free up space on the system drive where active virtual pagefiles reside.
Review application memory utilization maps using standard diagnostics.
Scale up system memory allocations if working with highly nested virtualized configurations.

description: The storage media is write-protected and cannot accept block modification calls.
fix: Verify physical write-protection switches are toggled off on SD cards or USB flash drives.
Remove write-protection attributes using diskpart or volume attribute commands.
Disable security configurations that enforce write locks on external media.

description: The target physical device is not ready to process incoming instructions.
fix: Confirm the storage media is fully inserted and mounted correctly.
Wait for system drives or virtual disks to initialize completely before execution.
Check the Device Manager to identify hardware device status issues.

description: A device attached to the system is not functioning.
fix: Update system drivers using official publisher packages.
Disconnect and re-seat physical interface connections.
Inspect the Windows System Event Viewer logs to evaluate hardware controller issues.

description: The process cannot access the file because it is being used by another process.
fix: Close the file in locking programs or terminate blocking utility processes.
Configure sharing parameters (FILE_SHARE_READ or FILE_SHARE_WRITE) in custom file calls.
Restart active background services if file handles remain locked.

description: The process cannot access the file because another process has locked a portion of the file.
fix: Ensure coordinate synchronization blocks are executed before accessing locked file regions.
Implement delay and retry logic in multi-threaded file read/write routines.
Release locked segments using standard file unlocking functions before proceeding.

description: The network or file request is not supported.
fix: Upgrade interface drivers and confirm compatibility on both host and target systems.
Use alternative programming APIs or protocol definitions.
Verify system capabilities match requirements of advanced features.

description: The network path was not found.
fix: Check host name or IP address spelling parameters within the path.
Verify system DNS configurations and confirm name resolution is functional.
Ensure server share properties remain active and accessible.

description: The file exists and cannot be overwritten without explicit override instructions.
fix: Specify an alternative name or directory location for file creation.
Enable overwrite attributes or recreate flags within calling code parameters.
Clean or rename conflicting target directories.

description: The parameter is incorrect. One or more arguments supplied to the system function contain invalid structures.
fix: Validate parameter structures and ranges against official documentation specifications.
Confirm parameters passed to script commands do not contain format or parsing errors.
Debug calling function structures to isolate null pointers and bounds issues.

description: There is not enough space on the disk.
fix: Free up storage space on the target volume by deleting unneeded data.
Clear system caches and temporary file systems.
Expand partition bounds using disk management utilities if virtualization models are active.

description: The semaphore timeout period has expired.
fix: Increase execution timeout thresholds inside client application settings.
Optimize local processing routines to reduce semaphore holding states.
Improve underlying storage and network transaction speeds to minimize execution wait states.

description: The data area passed to a system call is too small.
fix: Increase output memory allocations to handle peak query sizes.
Call system function twice: first to evaluate the necessary buffer size, and second to retrieve the output data.

description: The filename, directory name, or volume label syntax is incorrect.
fix: Remove illegal characters such as asterisks, question marks, and angle brackets.
Ensure length constraints fit within the MAX_PATH parameters.
Confirm directories do not contain invalid spaces or reserved naming terms.

description: The specified module could not be found.
fix: Confirm that the target dynamic link library file exists in standard execution paths.
Audit and repair missing secondary framework dependencies using parsing software.
Verify the application has access permissions to look up files inside the directory.

description: The specified procedure could not be found.
fix: Verify that the calling program requests an export symbol name matching binary descriptors.
Verify module versions align with targeted system libraries.
Ensure compiler target options include valid entrypoint mappings.

description: Cannot create a file when that file already exists.
fix: Ensure creation procedures check for target existence and handle exceptions smoothly.
Specify different filename variables for newly initiated file segments.
Clear temporary caches containing stale files with matching names.

description: The target is not a valid Win32 application.
fix: Confirm executable binaries are compiled to target the active CPU platform model.
Resolve possible binary file corruption by downloading replacement installer modules.
Avoid attempting directly executes on pure configuration text files.

description: Operation did not complete successfully because the file contains a virus or potentially unwanted software.
fix: Run complete diagnostic scans using security software.
If the target represents a safe custom binary, add directory path exceptions inside security suites.
Restore the file from clean master sources if corruption was confirmed.

description: More data is available from the system query.
fix: Configure loop structures to execute repeatedly until the return status shifts.
Double output parameters size maps to successfully absorb all data streams.

description: The wait operation timed out.
fix: Ensure concurrent processes are not experiencing deadlock conditions.
Increase API timeout parameters to align with actual task execution constraints.

description: User profile cannot be loaded.
fix: Restart the operating system to clear active file locks.
Check for active ProfileList registry corruption under registry keys.
Verify User Profile Service is active and configured correctly.

description: Arithmetic result exceeded 32 bits.
fix: Upgrade program variable logic to handle 64-bit integer values.
Add validation bounds-checking on mathematical calculations.

description: Application verifier has found an error in the current process.
fix: Inspect program threads using standard system verifier tools to isolate the exact lock or validation failure.
Fix invalid memory reference models in code modules.

description: Windows was unable to save all the data for the file. The data has been lost due to storage interface loss.
fix: Check storage array hardware connection interfaces.
Review network link status to remote fileshares.
Save the active file safely to a separate physical storage target.

description: The requested operation could not be completed due to a file system limitation.
fix: Defragment the target partition volume.
Avoid excessive file fragmentation by optimizing allocation block configurations.
Update application write logic to split huge metadata streams.

description: The requested operation requires elevated user privilege permissions.
fix: Execute the target process or installer path using elevated Administrator access.
Configure standard execution level flags inside compilation manifest scripts.

description: The I/O operation has been aborted because of either a thread exit or an application request.
fix: Investigate unexpected process or worker thread shutdowns.
Optimize communication loops to handle cancellation and shutdown flags gracefully.

description: Overlapped I/O operation is in progress.
fix: Implement standard callback functions or event handle polling patterns to manage completion states.

description: Recursion too deep; the stack overflowed.
fix: Verify recursive patterns in application code resolve correctly without creating infinite loops.
Convert deep recursion models into iterative loop designs.
Optimize stack requirements on application memory stacks.

description: The volume does not contain a recognized file system. Please make sure that all required file system drivers are loaded and that the volume is not corrupted.
fix: Confirm file system drivers matching the target storage are loaded.
Execute CHKDSK scans to evaluate partition integrity.
Format the drive using supported file systems (NTFS/exFAT) if data destruction is acceptable.

description: An attempt was made to reference a token that does not exist.
fix: Ensure thread impersonation processes execute successfully before requesting active tokens.
Verify calling user context mappings before executing access validation APIs.

description: The registry structure or image files are corrupt and unreadable.
fix: Run Deployment Image Servicing and Management (DISM) tools to restore system files.
Restore registry hive configurations using healthy shadow copies or system backups.

description: The service did not respond to the start or control request in a timely fashion.
fix: Increase default Windows services startup delay values in the registry.
Optimize code blocks inside the service entry functions to avoid long processing delays.
Check the Event Viewer to verify application-side dependency locks.

description: An instance of the service is already running.
fix: Identify duplicate start instructions inside scheduled task routines.
Confirm current service status using management terminals.

description: The service cannot be started, either because it is disabled or because it has no enabled devices associated with it.
fix: Adjust service Startup Type properties configuration to Automatic or Manual using services.msc.
Start the target service program using command terminal systems.

description: The specified service does not exist as an installed service.
fix: Check service name spelling parameters.
Re-register the service utilizing creation scripts or target installations utilities.

description: The service has not been started.
fix: Initiate the service program via net start commands.
Inspect configuration setup to determine startup failures.

description: The dependency service or group failed to start.
fix: Check services dependencies trees inside Services Console properties.
Locate and start parent service objects before running dependent targets.

description: The service did not start due to a logon failure.
fix: Update service credential configurations with accurate account profiles.
Confirm assigned service accounts hold adequate logon privileges in security settings.

description: A dynamic link library (DLL) initialization routine failed.
fix: Check for missing system framework components.
Verify target DLL dependencies are registered.
Inspect target initialize procedures in DLL scripts.

description: The request could not be performed because of an I/O device error.
fix: Inspect drive physical cabling.
Check storage health metrics using manufacturer utilities.
Verify device driver configuration parameter rules are intact.

description: Not enough server storage is available to process this command.
fix: Increase the IRPStackSize parameters value in registry configurations.
Reallocate memory limits within server shares.

description: No application is associated with the specified file for this operation.
fix: Assign default handler profiles to target file extensions.
Specify program executable targets within open instructions.

description: One of the library files needed to run this application cannot be found.
fix: Locate and place the necessary DLL in application deployment directories.
Reinstall the software application to recover library sets.

description: The device is not connected.
fix: Confirm network connections are active.
Reconnect local storage interfaces.

description: The network path was either typed incorrectly, does not exist, or the network provider is not currently available.
fix: Re-evaluate spelled net path syntax definitions.
Validate interface connection paths.

description: Multiple connections to a server or shared resource by the same user, using more than one user name, are not allowed.
fix: Disconnect prior active network drives configurations.
Utilize consistent credentials across shared server profiles.

description: The network is not present or not started.
fix: Enable local interface hardware.
Check network adapter settings.

description: The operation was canceled by the user.
fix: Handle cancellation events within runtime scripts cleanly.
Re-run actions without manual interventions.

description: The network location cannot be reached.
fix: Verify target node route tables.
Ensure local switches are online and forwarding packets.

description: This program is blocked by group policy.
fix: Modify app execution blocking parameters in Local Security policies.
Contact server system administrators to resolve group policy blocks.

description: The system detected an overrun of a stack-based buffer in this application.
fix: Debug memory code paths to enforce validation limits on inputs.
Apply secure buffer boundaries checks in program compilation scripts.

description: The service start failed since one or more services in the same process have an incompatible service SID type setting.
fix: Configure conflicting service settings to separate execution environments.
Update service SID types to support shared context modes.

description: An operation attempted to exceed an implementation-defined limit. Sequentially detailing all 16,000 system error codes exceeds the physical context output boundaries of the system.
fix: Query specific error ranges selectively.
Consult the Microsoft Error Lookup Tool for individual codes.
Examine [MS-ERREF] Windows Error Codes specifications for comprehensive lookups.

description: The requested file operation failed because the storage quota was exceeded.
fix: Request storage limit expansions from directory administrators.
Clean obsolete files from target user space paths.

description: There are currently no logon servers available to service the logon request.
fix: Verify connection link properties to the Active Directory domain controllers.
Confirm local DNS configurations resolved DC address parameters.

description: A required privilege is not held by the client.
fix: Adjust user group policy setups to assign required security permissions.
Run caller processes in elevated safety tokens configurations.

description: The user name or password is incorrect.
fix: Confirm login profiles characters are accurate.
Perform user account password resets if values were updated.

description: Account restrictions are preventing this user from signing in.
fix: Check if user account permissions are bound to specific time windows.
Verify account has a valid, non-blank password where enforced by system rules.

description: This user isn't allowed to sign in to this computer.
fix: Adjust Log On To parameters inside directory server user profiles.

description: The password for this account has expired.
fix: Update profile password settings on directory login terminals.

description: This user can't sign in because this account is currently disabled.
fix: Enable account controls inside Active Directory administration tools.

description: No mapping between account names and security IDs was done.
fix: Verify spelled group or user names conform to registry records.
Re-establish domain trust relationships configurations.

description: The access control list (ACL) structure is invalid.
fix: Re-evaluate security descriptions scripts formatting parameters.
Verify access flags match system formats.

description: The security ID structure is invalid.
fix: Confirm that SID strings conform to standard network structures.
Generate new SID markers using automated system utilities.

description: The file or directory is corrupted and unreadable.
fix: Execute disk repair routines using command prompt parameters.
Recover missing data sets from storage backups.

description: The disk structure is corrupted and unreadable.
fix: Replace failing storage components.
Run partition table reconstructions tools.

description: Insufficient system resources exist to complete the requested service.
fix: Increase desk space or virtual swap limits configuration.
Limit concurrent task threads executing in memory blocks.

description: The paging file is too small for this operation to complete.
fix: Verify storage sizes hosting Windows virtual pagefiles.
Extend memory capacities bounds in performance options.

description: This operation returned because the timeout period expired.
fix: Optimize hardware execution layers to process inputs quickly.
Adjust operation limit parameters inside application scripts.

description: The event log file is corrupted.
fix: Clear corrupted logs databases using administration terminals.
Rebuild targeted service structures.

description: The Windows Installer Service could not be accessed.
fix: Confirm installation framework is registered.
Start Windows Installer service manual settings.

description: Fatal error during installation.
fix: Review target execution permissions models on installers folders.
Locate and fix detailed path blocks listed in logs.

description: Another installation is already in progress.
fix: Wait for separate active installation programs to complete.
Terminate background running processes in task managers.

description: This installation package could not be opened.
fix: Confirm target file actually exists in specified path ranges.
Validate package integrity formats.

description: This installation is forbidden by system policy.
fix: Modify app restriction rules via Local Security Policies screens.

description: The RPC server is unavailable.
fix: Confirm host network links status are healthy.
Validate status of service configurations.

description: The remote procedure call failed.
fix: Ensure host processes are not crashing under load blocks.
Restart networking interfaces.

description: The trust relationship between this workstation and the primary domain failed.
fix: Re-join the local machine context inside directory domains.
Reset target computer profile objects on server panels.

description: Not enough quota is available to process this command.
fix: Analyze process memory loops for resource leaks.
Increase pool limits via registry adjustments.

description: The user's password must be changed before signing in.
fix: Update security password keys on login interfaces.

description: The referenced account is currently locked out.
fix: Wait for locked intervals periods to resolve naturally.
Unlock account flags using directory management consoles.

description: The specified color profile is invalid.
fix: Replace target profile formats with standard settings packages.

description: This network connection does not exist.
fix: Confirm network connection setups exist.
Map shares names appropriately.

description: The specified printer driver is currently in use.
fix: Remove active print queue items before updating.
Temporarily stop printing spooler services.

description: The requested operation is successful. Changes will not be effective until the system is rebooted.
fix: Trigger system reboot parameters immediately.

description: The DHCP client has obtained an IP address that is already in use on the network.
fix: Re-allocate local IP details using renew scripts.
Exclude allocated scopes inside DHCP parameters.

description: The instance name passed was not recognized as valid by a WMI data provider.
fix: Ensure proper instance variables conform to script definitions.

description: The file or directory is not a reparse point.
fix: Avoid symlink instructions on standard raw format files.

description: Secure Boot is not enabled on this machine.
fix: Enable Secure Boot parameter options inside UEFI setup windows.

description: The operation cannot be completed because other resources are dependent on this resource.
fix: Remove dependencies connections inside cluster managers screens.

description: The cluster resource is not online.
fix: Bring target resources online via clustering tools.

description: The group or resource is not in the correct state to perform the requested operation.
fix: Verify system cluster statuses before applying transitions instructions.

description: The specified file could not be encrypted.
fix: Ensure file systems support standard EFS components.
Verify system encryption certificates are properly created.

description: The specified file could not be decrypted.
fix: Confirm matching user certificates keys exist inside local profiles directories.

description: The requested encryption operation was blocked by policy.
fix: Adjust security parameters inside local administrative policy blocks.

description: The list of servers for this workgroup is not currently available.
fix: Enable network discovery parameters inside system panel configurations.
Verify computer browser service settings.

description: Log space is exhausted.
fix: Clear transactions logs databases manually.
Increase log partition sizes boundaries.

description: The requested operation was made in the context of a transaction that is no longer active.
fix: Initiate new transactions objects structures before performing write operations.

description: The function attempted to use a name that is reserved for use by another transaction.
fix: Close locking file sessions before starting overlapping operations.

description: The task you are trying to do can't be completed because Remote Desktop Services is currently busy.
fix: Wait for terminal session execution cycles to resolve.
Restart targeted services blocks.

description: An error occurred while installing the directory service.
fix: Verify Active Directory database folders permissions rules.
Check server event logs to isolate system installer errors.

description: The directory service is unavailable.
fix: Verify domain controller services are up and active.
Examine DNS network configurations.

description: Directory object not found.
fix: Verify exact spelling parameters of DN strings.
Confirm object creation replication has processed completely.

description: Replication access was denied.
fix: Assign security replication permissions models inside domain containers.

description: The DSA operation is unable to proceed because of a DNS lookup failure.
fix: Configure DNS pointer entries accurately inside name servers tables.
Clear client systems cache records.

description: Your computer could not be joined to the domain because you have exceeded the maximum number of computer accounts.
fix: Ask a domain administrator to register the computer account.
Reset user accounts creation limits values inside configuration variables.

description: DNS name does not exist.
fix: Verify target domain records exists inside lookup databases.
Clear host systems local DNS cache directories.

description: No records found for given DNS query.
fix: Confirm queried records types are correctly published.

description: No DNS servers configured for local system.
fix: Configure active DNS IP locations inside target network adapters panels.

description: An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions.
fix: Confirm firewall rules do not restrict specific ports paths.
Launch sockets services using elevated administrative rights.

description: Only one usage of each socket address is normally permitted.
fix: Stop concurrent processes locking identical target ports configurations.
Utilize alternative ports scopes for active socket connections.

description: An existing connection was forcibly closed by the remote host.
fix: Examine remote software services execution paths for system failures.
Re-establish connections structures manually.

description: A connection attempt failed because the connected party did not properly respond after a period of time.
fix: Confirm targeted endpoints are actively processing status loops.
Adjust firewall security parameters to allow direct passage.

description: No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it.
fix: Confirm receiving port listener procedures are actively running.
Validate connection port profiles entries.

description: No such host is known.
fix: Confirm targeted network hosts names entries are correct.
Update DNS routing profiles configurations.

description: No more internet handles can be allocated.
fix: Release stale connection objects handles cleanly.
Configure resource recycle loops inside programs profiles.

description: The server name or address could not be resolved.
fix: Verify network interface parameters paths are connected to active gateways.
Check server names spelling accuracy.

description: The attempt to connect to the server failed.
fix: Confirm target site configurations port numbers match active rules.
Verify system firewall transit pathways are open.

description: The server response could not be parsed.
fix: Analyze target server program logic outputs.
Verify standard headers formats match communication layouts definitions.

description: The specified quick mode policy already exists.
fix: Configure active connection profiles to utilize current IPsec structures.
Delete redundant entries inside IPsec management consoles.

description: The specified quick mode policy was not found.
fix: Construct missing IPsec connection policy parameters targets.
Verify policy spelling patterns match registered references.

description: An advanced installer failed during setup or servicing.
fix: Examine servicing framework files structures using SFC tools.
Examine active setups variables inside servicing CBS logs documents.

description: The character encoding in the XML declaration did not match the encoding used in the document.
fix: Correct character declarations headers inside XML sources to align with document contents.

description: The identities of the manifests are identical but their contents are different.
fix: Verify side-by-side component configurations matches manifest specifications parameters.

description: The component identities are different.
fix: Ensure manifest configuration parameters match identical deployment identities.

description: The specified channel path is invalid.
fix: Confirm spelled channels directories paths conform to active logs folders definitions.

description: The specified query is invalid.
fix: Verify query filter parsing parameters match active logging standards.

description: The publisher metadata cannot be found in the resource.
fix: Ensure that log provider drivers resources are fully registered.

description: The template for an event definition cannot be found in the resource.
fix: Reinstall system components assemblies structures to recover templates maps.
